title: Comments & Formatting 📝
description: Learn to write clean, readable code with comments.

slides.title: Leaving Notes 📝
slides.content:
As you write more code, things can get complicated. 

It is incredibly helpful to leave notes for yourself (and others) explaining what the code is doing. We call these notes **Comments**.

slides.title: The Invisible Ink 🕵️‍♀️
slides.content:
## How to Comment

The beautiful thing about Comments is that **the computer completely ignores them**.

They exist entirely for human eyes. In Java, there are two common ways to write comments.

slides.title: Single-Line Comments 📏
slides.content: Use two forward slashes `//` for a comment that spans only one line.

quizData.question: What is the correct way to make a single-line comment in Java?
quizData.explanation: Single-line comments start with two forward slashes: `//`.

slides.title: Multi-Line Comments 📚
slides.content: If you need to write a long paragraph, you can use multi-line comments. Start with `/*` and end with `*/`.

quizData.question: What happens when Java encounters a comment?

options.text: It prints the comment to the screen.
It completely ignores it and skips to the next line of real code.

quizData.explanation: Comments are completely skipped by the Java Compiler. They are only there to help the humans reading the code!

slides.title: Readability is Key 🔑
slides.content:
## Good Formatting

Notice how the `main` method is indented inside the `Class`, and the `println` is indented inside the `main` method? 

Java doesn't care about spaces and indentation, but humans DO. Always properly format your code with indentations so it's easy to read the "nesting" structure.
